competitor_patterns: Bikerumor's #1-ranking post at 24,900 words is 6-7x longer than our longest posts (~4,000 words), with continuously updated editor notes (last update May 2026) signalling freshness to Google — their snippet explicitly calls out the update date

Top competitors structure content with named product H2s (e.g. 'Julbo Density', 'Oakley Sutro Lite') acting as individual mini-reviews within a roundup, satisfying both head-term and long-tail product queries in a single URL

All top-3 competitors include explicit 'How we test' or 'Why trust us' credibility sections — Cyclist.co.uk uses 'How do we test and why trust Cyclist's advice?' — which boosts E-E-A-T signals Velluto content currently lacks

Bikerumor and Cyclist.co.uk both carry comparison tables AND FAQ schema AND real user/editor reviews in the same post — the triple combination dominates rich-result eligibility; our best pages still miss at least one of the three

Competitor snippets consistently lead with a concrete, dated number ('18 sunglasses tested', 'updated May 21 2026') in the meta description, which drives CTR even from positions 4-8 — our meta descriptions are benefit-led rather than data-led
our_gaps: Word count ceiling: our pillar posts cap at ~4,000 words vs Bikerumor's 24,900 — we are not comprehensive enough to win the 'best cycling glasses 2026' head term; Google rewards depth and coverage breadth on commercial-investigation queries

No 'tested by' or first-person ride-condition credibility layer: competitor content names specific routes, weather conditions, and ride distances to prove real-world testing; our posts reference features but lack experiential proof that satisfies E-E-A-T

FAQ schema coverage is only 33% across our posts — 26 out of ~40 pages have no FAQ schema at all, directly limiting our eligibility for Google's FAQ rich results and AI Overview citations on near-top keywords

Zero standalone interchangeable-lens deep-dive despite 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' being a keyword where Alibaba ranks #1 — a clear authority vacuum we have product-level advantage to fill but haven't exploited with sufficient depth

Our Oakley-alternatives cluster (3 separate URLs) is cannibalising itself: 'oakley alternatives' sits at pos 6.3 with 284 impressions but only 9 clicks, suggesting Google is splitting equity across near-duplicate pages rather than consolidating one authoritative result
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 — Alibaba ranks #1 (signals weak editorial competition); Velluto's click-in tool-free system is a direct product match; no current post exceeds 1,712 words on this topic

photochromic vs interchangeable lens cycling glasses — a high-intent comparative query not directly targeted by any top competitor; bridges our two existing thin posts (photochromic 1,104w and interchangeable 1,153w) into one authoritative head-to-head
cycling glasses 25g ultralight — a spec-specific long-tail with near-zero competition where Velluto is the exact answer; ultra-specific queries convert well and seed AI-answer citations

road cycling glasses anti fog hot weather 2026 — summer timing is perfect (July 2026); we have three anti-fog posts but none specifically targeting hot-weather climbers vs cold-weather descenders, missing a seasonal search surge

cycling glasses narrow face interchangeable lenses — combines two underserved intents (fit + lens system) where our adjustable nose pads + VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione lenses give a concrete answer competitors cannot match
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 6,000 words for any new pillar post on head terms ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'best cycling sunglasses 2026') — include at least 5 named product H2 sections to capture long-tail product queries within the same URL, matching Bikerumor's structural depth

Open every post with a datestamped editor note ('Last tested: July 2026 — X rides, Y conditions') to match competitor freshness signals in snippets; this single change can lift CTR from positions 5-8 by surfacing a date in the Google snippet

Add a 'How we tested' H2 section (100-150 words) to every new post naming specific ride conditions, distances, and weather — this directly addresses the E-E-A-T gap vs Cyclist.co.uk and Bikerumor and is required for Google AI Overview citation eligibility

Every new post must include FAQ schema AND a comparison table AND at least one inline review quote — all three together, not two of three; posts with all three currently represent our highest-CTR pages (up to 4.1%)

Write meta descriptions in the format '[Number] tested, updated [Month Year] — [specific differentiator]' e.g. '7 glasses tested, July 2026 — only one under 30g with tool-free lenses' to match competitor data-led snippet patterns that drive CTR from mid-page positions

For the 'oakley alternatives' cluster: consolidate content signals by internally linking all three Oakley-alternative URLs to a single canonical 'best oakley alternatives for cyclists 2026' hub page, and expand that hub to 5,000+ words with individual H2s per alternative brand — this will resolve the cannibalization suppressing position 6.3

Anchor every post's value argument to at least two StradaPro-specific specs with exact numbers (25g frame weight, 30-day trial, two named lens SKUs) rather than generic benefit language — specific numbers are what AI Overview systems extract and cite
seo_quick_wins: Expand the 10 posts currently between 1,030-1,350 words to a minimum of 2,500 words by adding a 'What our testing found' section, a comparison table (if missing), and 3-5 FAQ items — this addresses both the word-count gap vs competitors and the FAQ schema gap in a single editing pass

Add an explicit 'Last updated: [date]' timestamp visible in the page body (not just meta) to every post published before July 2026, and re-submit to Google Search Console — this triggers re-crawl and surfaces the freshness date in snippets for the 'best X 2026' queries where dates visibly improve CTR
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store 2026 | Why: 'veluto' is a misspelling of the brand; the current result likely shows a generic page title; correcting to the brand name + year + 'Official Store' reassures searchers they've found the right brand, lifting CTR from 0.8% — also add a 301 redirect or hreflang note catching the misspelling

best cycling glasses → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026 — Tested & Ranked | Why: Current position 8.4 means CTR is suppressed; adding 'Tested & Ranked' mirrors Bikerumor's highest-CTR title pattern and the year signals freshness within the 60-char limit

best oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026 — Lighter & Cheaper | Why: Matches the existing URL slug 'lighter-cheaper' which already performed well; the specific benefit promise ('Lighter & Cheaper') differentiates from generic roundup titles at position 7.3 and directly addresses the searcher's intent to save money without losing quality

oakley alternative → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 — Top 5 | Why: Position 6.3 with only 1.6% CTR means the title isn't compelling enough; adding 'Top 5' creates a listicle expectation that consistently outperforms generic titles at mid-page positions, within 60 chars
content_quick_wins: velluto (pos 4.7) → Topic: Velluto StradaPro — Full Brand & Product Hub Page | Add: Consolidate brand signals onto the homepage or a dedicated /stradapro/ landing page with structured data (Product schema with price, weight, lens names), a 'Why Velluto' trust section, and internal links to all review posts — position 4.7 for your own brand name is underperforming; a strong brand hub with Product schema can push this to top 2

oakley alternatives (pos 6.3) → Topic: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 — The Definitive Guide | Add: Merge the three competing Oakley-alternative URLs into one 5,000+ word canonical post with individual H2 sections per alternative (Tifosi, POC, Roka, Velluto), a weight/price/lens comparison table, FAQ schema targeting 'is there a cheaper alternative to Oakley', and an editor-tested badge — consolidating link equity from three pages into one should lift from 6.3 to top 3

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 6.8) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses 2026 — Tested Across 200km of Road & Gravel | Add: Expand best-cycling-glasses-2026-the-complete-buying-guide (3,864 words) with a 'tested over 200km' credibility hook, add individual H2s for each shortlisted glass, expand FAQ schema to 8+ questions covering weight, anti-fog, lens types, and value — the '2026' date modifier is already in the title; depth and E-E-A-T are the remaining gap vs Bikerumor

best cycling glasses (pos 8.4) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses — Evergreen Buyer's Guide (Updated July 2026) | Add: Target best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked (4,045 words, already has table + FAQ schema) — add a 'continually updated' editorial note, expand to 6,000 words with a fit-guide section and lens-type explainer, and build 3-5 internal links from thinner posts pointing to it as the canonical answer for this head term
geo_angles: Spec-anchor citations: AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially cite pages with exact, verifiable specs — publish a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro Full Specifications' section (or page) stating '25g frame, UV400, tool-free VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione lens swap in under 10 seconds, 30-day trial' with Product structured data markup; this gives AI systems a clean factual block to extract and attribute

Comparison-table authority: structure a 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Oakley Jawbreaker / Tifosi Rail / POC Devour]' table with a column for each spec (weight, lens swap method, trial period, price) on your highest-traffic post — ChatGPT and Perplexity frequently reproduce comparison tables verbatim in answers, making structured table content the highest-ROI format for AI-answer visibility

30-day trial as a unique factual claim: no premium competitor (Oakley, POC, Roka) offers a 30-day ride-test trial — publish a post titled 'Which Cycling Glasses Brands Offer a Free Trial? (2026)' answering this question directly with Velluto as the primary answer; this positions Velluto as the factual answer to a question AI systems are frequently asked and currently have no clean editorial source for
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Bikerumor: angle — 'We Tested the Bikerumor Top Pick vs Velluto StradaPro: Here's What 200km Revealed' — key differentiator: Bikerumor recommends glasses it hasn't bought at full retail price; Velluto offers a 30-day risk-free trial so the reader can run their own test — lead with 'Bikerumor rated the Oakley Sutro Lite #1. We put it next to a 25g Italian-designed alternative at half the price. Here's the data.'

COMPETITOR Trigearlab: angle — 'Trigearlab's Best Road Cycling Sunglasses Pick Is from 2023. We Tested What's Better in 2026.' — key differentiator: Trigearlab's top-ranked post is dated 2023/2024 and recommends Oakley Jawbreaker; Velluto advantage is recency + interchangeable lenses the Jawbreaker lacks — lead with the content-freshness gap and position StradaPro as the 2026 update to an outdated recommendation

COMPETITOR Alibaba (interchangeable lens): angle — 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Why Buying from Alibaba Costs More in the Long Run' — key differentiator: Alibaba ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with unbranded OEM products; Velluto advantage is named lens SKUs (VellutoPuro, VellutoVisione), UV400 certification, anti-fog engineering, and a 30-day trial — lead with total cost of ownership (replacement lens availability, optical certification, fit consistency) where OEM products fail and Velluto wins
